subject: Re: M Crashes during species import
body:
Hi Hee Jong,

unfortunately Windows 8.1 vs. 10 on a Linux host is outside of what I can 
test here. For the latest error, was there anything written in the command 
line windows that M's refinement spawned?

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, September 11, 2020 at 1:08:24 AM UTC+2 Hee Jong Kim wrote:

> Hi Dimitry,
>
> It turns out, under the linux base windows VM environment, Warp is happy 
> with win 8.1 while M is much happier with win 10.
> After switching to win 10, i reach to "Performing refinement" and it 
> crashed with wealth amount of error msg.
> It would be awesome if you can tell the source of the issue and how to get 
> around or resolve it.
>
> Please find the attached screenshot.
>
> Thanks.
>
> best,
> heejong
>
> [image: Screen Shot 2020-09-05 at 1.10.26 AM.png]
> On Thursday, September 3, 2020 at 4:24:20 AM UTC-4 Hee Jong Kim wrote:
>
>> Hi Dimitry,
>>
>> Thanks for the guidance.
>> One of the many issues from my side was unstable windows system.
>> Warp was repeatedly crash and M was acting weird so I revisited the 
>> windows system again and finally figured out (so far) windows 8.1 is the 
>> best when I need to passthrough GPUs from linux to windows.
>> I found out this was one of the major cause when I tried to import warp 
>> star file to cryosparc and relion, suggesting that some of mrcs files are 
>> missing even though they are present in the star file.
>>
>> After without a single crash, warp perfectly produced all mrcs and star 
>> file and I was able to import them into cryosparc and relion with no issue.
>>
>> So, I move onto M by importing data sources and species (500px still 
>> crashing so I had to stick with 400px with 1.08A/px without binning)
>> Now with default setting of refine with 6x4 instead of 3x3 to match the 
>> dimension, with the both current official version and nightly version, it 
>> hangs during "Figuring out memory capacity..." step. I started with 
>> multiple warpworker windows and doing some stuff and all warpworker windows 
>> disappeared. 
>>
>> After that, cpu, ram, disk, network, and gpu usages are minimal.
>> I see M.exe in both gpu #0 and #1 still via nvidia-smi and little bit of 
>> fluctuation of GPU-Util percentage somewhere between 7% to 16% in #0 while 
>> complete 0% in #1.
>>
>> I don't see any crash logs so it's a little bit difficult to guess what M 
>> is up to.
>>
>> If you have any insight on how to follow up on this, please I'm all ear.
>>
>> Thank you.
>>
>> best,
>> heejong
>>
>> On Thursday, August 13, 2020 at 8:09:49 AM UTC-4 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Hee Jong,
>>>
>>> there is no need to go all the way back to Warp to enable binning. 
>>> During refinement, data are automatically scaled to the pixel size of your 
>>> species. Just rescale the maps and mask (e.g. with Relion's 
>>> relion_image_handler) before species import. Don't forget to import the 
>>> particles using the pixel size they were refined with (looks like 1.08 A/px 
>>> in this case).
>>>
>>> I'll add a button to enable binning of already imported species in the 
>>> next version. You can almost do it now by rescaling the maps and changing 
>>> the pixel size parameter in the .species file, but then the denoiser won't 
>>> be retrained.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>>
>>> On Thursday, August 6, 2020 at 1:40:02 AM UTC+2, Hee Jong Kim wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hi Linhua Tai,
>>>>
>>>> Thanks for the response.
>>>> Here's my answers for your questions.
>>>>
>>>> 1. the current box size is 400 in 1.08A/px
>>>> 2. I will take a look into it. Thanks for the suggestion.
>>>> 3. so, resacle the mrc files along with data.star files and put it in 
>>>> the species? Or should I bin it and run the refinement job and take them 
>>>> into the species?
>>>>
>>>> Thank you!
>>>>
>>>> best,
>>>> heejong kim
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Tuesday, August 4, 2020 at 11:48:37 AM UTC-4 888...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi Kim,
>>>>> there are several things I suggest you to do,
>>>>> 1. could you state how big your box size is? That should give us a 
>>>>> rough idea.
>>>>> 2. About m worker crash problem, it could come from too close-to-edge 
>>>>> particles, please clean up your data star file and give it an another try.
>>>>> 3. About binning, I think maybe you can rescale on the data.star 
>>>>> level, and change the according map and parameters accordingly in adding 
>>>>> species step?
>>>>> Let me know if some of these helps.
>>>>>
>>>>> Yours sincerely,
>>>>> Linhua Tai
>>>>>
>>>>> On Sunday, August 2, 2020 at 2:17:14 AM UTC-4, Hee Jong Kim wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Turns out it's due to particle box size is too big for the gpu 
>>>>>> (2080Ti)
>>>>>> After recentering and shrinking the box size, i don't get this error 
>>>>>> msg anymore.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> However, unfortunately, now the warpWorker crash & M's indefinite 
>>>>>> idle issue came to me.
>>>>>> It emits length error msg in the warpworker window and closes 
>>>>>> immediately. Unfortunately, worker crash log only capture's cuda memory 
>>>>>> error.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I suspect that the box size is still too big for the refinement.
>>>>>> If i want to bin the particles to perform the refinement, do I have 
>>>>>> to go all the way back to Warp to bin first and come back with adjusted 
>>>>>> coordinates and all that?
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Thanks,
>>>>>> heejong
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Friday, July 31, 2020 at 4:02:43 PM UTC-4 Hee Jong Kim wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Turns out this happened during the 3D denoising so 1 gpu wasn't 
>>>>>>> enough to provide all the memory. 
>>>>>>> So, I added one more by gpu passthru and it successfully passed.
>>>>>>> However, I'm getting the new crash msg during the refinement. (with 
>>>>>>> multiple pop-up so lastcrash.txt cannot capture the log properly)
>>>>>>> Here's the screenshot of the crash msg.
>>>>>>> [image: Screenshot 2020-07-31 12.58.57.png][image: Screenshot 
>>>>>>> 2020-07-31 14.43.22.png][image: 20200731.png]
>>>>>>> Any thoughts, insights, and suggestions are greatly appreciated.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thanks.
>>>>>>> hee jong
>>>>>>> On Thursday, July 30, 2020 at 7:18:33 PM UTC-4 Hee Jong Kim wrote:
>>>>>>>
